title: #437 Fredens Vägar
summary_md:
# #437 Fredens Vägar

**Feed**: market-makers  
**Published**: 2026-06-18  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode centers on the de-escalation of the Iran/Hormuz crisis, which the hosts see as bullish for equities but bearish for oil as supply normalizes and OPEC discipline frays, plus a shift in macro worry from energy shocks toward inflation and a possible Fed/Riksbank rate hike. They cover frothy risk appetite around the SpaceX IPO (low float, looming lockup expiries) and present two Swedish small-cap cases: Terranor (road maintenance riding a structural surge in state funding) and Upsales/AppSales (a CRM player de-pressed after a founder-CEO sale and Aira spin-off).

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 TERR — Terranor | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Structural surge in Swedish road-maintenance funding (+>50% over 4 yrs), strong order book, depressed by PE forced-seller block now cleared; trades EV/EBITDA ~5 on 2026.

#### Reasoning

State road-maintenance budgets rise from +1.5bn (2025) to +6bn (2028), giving long visibility via 4-year state contracts. Terranor is #2 behind state-owned Svevia in a low-competition niche. A PE firm dumped its 45% stake in one block (stock +30% since), removing overhang. Finnish loss-making contract dispute capped at ~80m SEK (6 months profit) and ex-CEO's Serneke baggage are seen as transient. Adjusted EBITDA 98m gives EV/EBITDA ~7 trailing, ~5 on 2026 guidance vs sector ~12; >100% upside if 

#### Sell Triggers

- if 2026 guidance is missed
- if subcontractors raise prices and compress margins
- if state road-maintenance budget increases are reversed
- if the Finnish contract ruling exceeds ~80m SEK
- margin is thin so any downturn could force a rights issue

> *"i så fall skulle vi vara uppsida på över 100%. Det har varit lite insiderköp nu under våren. ... Så jag äger aktier."*

---

### 🟢 UPSALE — Upsales (AppSales) | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 60%

CRM firm down ~30% YTD/~10% on CEO change; founder Wikberg sold to focus on Aira spin-off, replaced by Johan Kallblad. Post-Aira margins jump, sell-off seen as overdone.

#### Reasoning

Founder-CEO Daniel Wikberg stepped down to run spun-off, pre-revenue Aira and sold ~75% of his stake, triggering a ~10% drop and ~30% YTD decline. Host argues the sale reflects focus, not loss of faith, and new CEO Kallblad (ex-Excitec, owns shares, 15-yr relationship) is a positive. Aira was pure cost (~6m/quarter); once removed, EBITDA margin jumps from 17% to >35% guided from Q2, with 10-15% revenue growth. Stock has fallen more than Aira's value; ~PE 12-13 / EV/EBITDA 7-8 seen as slightly ch

#### Sell Triggers

- if the >35% EBITDA margin guidance from Q2 is not met
- if ARR keeps stalling beyond seasonal Q1 churn
- if the new CEO sells or departs
- if AI-driven CRM disruption materially erodes the customer base

> *"mitt bett här är ju på att det där är överdrivet helt enkelt. Att det är inte så att han säljer på grund av att han tror att det kommer gå dåligt."*

---

### ⛔ SPACEX — SpaceX | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 60%

IPO up 60-70% on record retail buying and very low float; ~50% of shares are early-release/lockup with ~20% sellable from Q1 report (August), setting up heavy insider selling.

#### Reasoning

The rally is driven by extreme low float and lockups preventing existing holders from selling, plus Musk's ability to talk up the stock; ex-Goldman CEO Blankfein called it expensive at $100bn. Early-release shares (~50% of total) begin unlocking at the first quarterly report in August (~20%) and after a 30% move above IPO price (~5% more), so insiders can dump while retail keeps buying. Hosts call it 'an extremely good cocktail for it to go really badly from the next report onward' and one sold 

#### Sell Triggers

- lockup/early-release shares unlocking at the August Q1 report
- insider selling once shares clear 30% above IPO price
- any cooling of retail buying given the thin float
- the Cursor acquisition closing and diluting/altering the story

> *"Det finns väl extremt bra cocktail för att det ska gå riktigt jävla dåligt från nästa rapport och framåt."*

---
